docs.title: Orders Manager One For All
docs.text:
Orders Manager One For All is a professional all-in-one trading panel for MetaTrader 5, designed for both retail traders and prop firm environments, it also supports cent accounts for either Netting or Hedging.

It replaces the default MT5 order interface with a modern, CCanvas-based UI that gives you full control over risk management, order placement, and position monitoring — all from a single panel.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-panel UI — Main Panel, Easy Order (EO) and Order List (LO) work together seamlessly
  • Risk-based sizing — calculate lot size automatically from % risk, fixed amount or manual lots
  • Multi-TP support — up to 4 take profits per order with individual % allocation (Hedging accounts)
  • Synthetic Netting Ladder — multi-TP simulation for Netting accounts via partial closes
  • One-Click mode — instant market orders with predefined SL/TP and volume
  • Trade Badges — visual SL/TP/Entry overlays on the chart, fully interactive
  • Breakeven automation — move SL to entry with optional commission offset
  • Bulk actions — close all, partial close (25/50/75%), breakeven all in one click
  • Multi-language — interface available in English, Spanish, Portuguese and Russian
  • Broker-agnostic — works on Standard, Cent, Hedging and Netting accounts

docs.title: Panels Overview
docs.text: Panel Purpose Main Panel Account info, quick actions, options Easy Order (EO) Visual order placement with draggable SL/TP lines Order List (LO) Live position and pending order management

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• MetaTrader 5 (build 3000+)
  • Any broker account type (Standard, Cent, Hedging, Netting)
  • Tested on Hedging and Netting accounts

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: New to the EA? Head to the Getting Started guide for installation and first steps.

docs.title: Support & Community
docs.text:
Channel Link 📖 Wiki & Docs You're already here! 🛒 MQL5 Marketplace Product page 📺 YouTube Coming Soon... 📢 Telegram Channel (Updates) t.me/OrdersManagerOneForAllUpdates 💬 Telegram Group (Support) t.me/OrdersManagerOneForAllSupport

Tip

Follow the Telegram Channel for update announcements, and join the Telegram Group for setup help, bug reports and community discussion.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable features and important fixes for Orders Manager One For All.

docs.title: v1.88
docs.text:
Feature: Diagnostics button

New [Diagnostics] button at the bottom of the OPTIONS section in the Main Panel. Click it to print a full troubleshooting snapshot to the MT5 Journal/Experts tab, including EA state, account type, symbol info, MT5 build, all open positions (with type, lots, entry, SL, TP, P&L), pending orders, and active Netting Ladders. Sensitive financial data (balance, equity, margin, login) is intentionally excluded so the output can be shared safely with support.

To save or share the log: in the Experts tab, right-click a row → Open to view the full log file, or select rows and use Copy to paste into a support message.

Improvement: Full English translation of all internal messages

All internal messages, warnings and feedback strings are now in English across all modules. Fixes also apply to user-visible UI strings that were not yet going through the translation system (TR()), including order list pagination and runner leg labels.

Fix: Three panel buttons silently unresponsive

The hit detection array in the Main Panel had a hard limit of 16 slots. With 19 buttons registered, the last three (Diagnostics, Include Commissions in Risk, and the Reverse Mode toggle) were silently dropped and never responded to hover or click. Array increased to 24 slots.

Fix: Easy Order and Order List sections misaligned in OPTIONS

When the language toggle row was added to the GENERAL card in a previous version, the vertical positions of the Easy Order and Order List sub-sections were not updated to match. This caused both sections to overlap with the GENERAL card. Layout formulas corrected.

docs.title: v1.87
docs.text:
Fix: Wiki help button [?] opens URL in panel

New [?] button in the Main Panel header. Clicking it displays the wiki URL in an MT5 alert popup and prints it to the Journal for easy copying.

Fix: NettingLadder file isolation and input validation

Ladder persistence files moved from MQL5/Files/ root to MQL5/Files/EO_netting/ subdirectory. Added range validation when loading files to reject corrupt or manually edited data.

Improvement: EA renamed to "Orders Manager One For All"

File, EA name constant, and panel header updated to reflect the official product name.

docs.title: v1.85
docs.text:
Improvement: Input settings reorganized

All EA input parameters are now organized into 6 clearly labeled sections ([GC], [TB], [EO], [OC], [MP], [LO]), making the Properties window easier to navigate. All descriptions rewritten in English.

docs.title: v1.84
docs.text:
Feature: Portuguese (PT-BR) and Russian language support

Added Portuguese (Brazil) and Russian to the multi-language system. The language toggle in the panel now shows [EN] [ES] [PT] [RU].

docs.title: v1.83
docs.text:
Feature: Multi-language interface (EN / ES)

The full panel UI (Main Panel, Easy Order, Order List) can now be switched between English and Spanish in real time. Language preference persists across MT5 restarts. Toggle available in OPTIONS → GENERAL.

docs.title: v1.82
docs.text:
Fix: Chart objects left behind after removing the EA

When the EA was removed while the Easy Order panel was open, draggable lines and input fields could remain visible on the chart. This has been fully resolved with a three-layer cleanup on EA removal.

docs.title: v1.81
docs.text:
Fix: Incorrect lot volume display on instruments with small volume step

On instruments like BTCUSD where the broker's minimum volume step is 0.001, the lot field was showing 0.00 instead of the correct value. The display now adapts its decimal precision to the instrument's volume step.

docs.title: v1.80
docs.text:
Feature: Slippage protection for market orders

New InpMaxSlippagePts input. When set, the EA checks the price movement between order calculation and execution — if the price moves more than the allowed threshold, the order is aborted and a warning is shown in the panel. Set to 0 to disable (default).

docs.title: v1.72
docs.text:
Improvement: Netting Ladder stability and persistence

Intermediate TP rungs now survive MT5 restarts and symbol changes. Added desync detection: if the user manually edits the broker TP, the ladder detects the change and resyncs automatically.

docs.title: v1.70
docs.text:
Feature: Synthetic Netting Ladder (multi-TP for Netting accounts)

Netting accounts only allow one position per symbol, so multi-TP is implemented via automatic partial closes. The EA monitors the position and executes each rung at the right price. The Order List shows the next active rung in the TP column. Intermediate rung badges are shown on the chart alongside the main TP badge.

docs.title: v1.63
docs.text:
Improvement: R:R moved from Entry badge to TP badge

The Risk:Reward ratio is now displayed on the TP badge (where it makes more sense) instead of the Entry badge. The TP badge also shows estimated profit. R:R updates live while dragging the TP line on the chart.

docs.title: v1.60
docs.text:
Feature: Multi-TP support — up to 4 Take Profits per order (Hedging)

On Hedging accounts, you can now set up to 4 independent Take Profit levels, each with its own percentage allocation of the total volume. Each TP leg is sent as a separate position with its own badge on the chart. The TP % fields auto-redistribute when you toggle legs on or off.

docs.title: v1.56
docs.text:
Improvement: Panels auto-scale to chart width

All three panels now adapt their font and layout scale to the actual chart window size at startup, preventing overflow or truncation on narrow charts.

docs.title: v1.54
docs.text:
Feature: Order type filter in the Order List

A new dropdown next to the view tabs lets you filter by order type: All, Buy, Sell, Buy Limit, Sell Limit, Buy Stop, Sell Stop. The filter resets automatically when switching between Open / Pending / All tabs. Bulk actions (close, partial close, breakeven) respect the active filter.

docs.title: v1.43
docs.text:
Feature: Multi-symbol filter in the Order List

A symbol dropdown in the Order List lets you view and manage positions and orders across all symbols from a single panel, not just the chart's symbol. Bulk actions apply only to the selected symbol (or all symbols if "All" is chosen). The selected symbol is saved and restored across MT5 restarts.

docs.title: v1.27
docs.text:
Feature: One-Click mode in Easy Order

A new mode that lets you place instant market orders with a single click using predefined volume, SL and TP. Includes [−] / [+] volume buttons, Volume Step selector, and the same feedback flash as Manual mode.

docs.title: v1.22
docs.text:
Feature: Reverse Mode for Breakeven (SL placement option)

When using the Breakeven action, you can now choose between two behaviors: - SL at original distance — mirrors the SL symmetrically past entry - SL at original entry — moves SL exactly to the entry price

Toggle available in OPTIONS → ORDER LIST.

docs.title: v1.20
docs.text:
Feature: Trade Badges — chart overlays for every position

Visual badges appear on the right side of the chart for each open position and pending order, showing Entry price, SL and TP with estimated P&L, lot size and R:R. Quick action buttons (+SL, +TP) place levels automatically using ATR-based distance. Badge colors and sizes are fully configurable.

docs.title: v1.18
docs.text:
Feature: Bulk actions in the Order List

New action buttons in the Order List header: close all (with optional double-click confirmation), partial close at 25% / 50% / 75%, and breakeven all — applied to all visible positions in the active filter.

docs.title: v1.14
docs.text:
Feature: Mini menu when the panel is minimized

When the Main Panel is minimized to its FAB button, a compact mini menu appears with quick access to the Easy Order and Order List panels — no need to expand the panel first.

docs.title: v1.12
docs.text:
Feature: Account type display (Hedging / Netting)

The account card in the Main Panel now shows the account margin mode (Hedging or Netting), making it easy to confirm which mode is active before placing orders.

docs.title: v1.07
docs.text:
Feature: OPTIONS section collapse / expand with animation

The OPTIONS section in the Main Panel can now be collapsed to save screen space, with a smooth animated transition. The collapsed/expanded state persists across MT5 restarts.

docs.title: v1.06
docs.text:
Feature: Full session persistence

All panel settings (language, toggles, EasyOrder configuration, selected tabs, panel positions, collapsed sections) are now saved and restored automatically across MT5 restarts, timeframe changes and EA reloads.

docs.title: v1.01
docs.text:
Improvement: Order List Panel rewritten with CCanvas

The Order List was rebuilt from scratch using a single CCanvas bitmap, replacing dozens of individual MT5 objects. Result: zero flickering, instant drag, lower CPU usage and support for hover effects and pixel-precise layouts.

docs.title: v0.17
docs.text:
Feature: Animated minimize button (FAB)

The minimize button animates between its header position (expanded) and a floating action button position (minimized), with a smooth easing transition.

docs.title: v0.16
docs.text:
Feature: Minimize / maximize with curtain animation

The Main Panel can be minimized to a single floating button with a smooth curtain animation (ease-out cubic). Expand is faster than minimize for snappier UX.

docs.title: v0.14
docs.text:
Feature: UIPanel and EasyOrder migrated to CCanvas

Both panels rebuilt using CCanvas bitmaps — eliminating flickering caused by individual MT5 objects. DPI-aware scaling, smooth hover effects and pixel-precise layout are now possible. EasyOrder lines are also badged with CCanvas overlays showing lot size, R:R and risk amount.

docs.title: v0.7
docs.text:
Feature: Easy Order panel with draggable SL / TP / Entry lines

The Easy Order panel lets you place orders by dragging SL, TP and Entry lines directly on the chart. Risk is calculated automatically based on your chosen mode (% of balance, fixed amount, or manual lots). Orders can be placed as Market, Limit or Stop.

docs.title: v0.6
docs.text:
Feature: Individual position management

Each open position now has its own row in the Main Panel with close buttons (25% / 50% / 75% / full close), entry price, SL, TP and individual P&L.

docs.title: v0.5
docs.text:
Feature: Quick actions — Close All, Breakeven, Partial Close

Added Close All (with optional include pending orders toggle), BE All (move all SLs to breakeven, with optional commission offset), and 25% / 50% / 75% partial close buttons.

docs.title: v0.2
docs.text:
Feature: Risk-based lot size calculation

The EA automatically calculates the correct lot size from a percentage of balance (or equity / free margin), SL distance and instrument type. Supports Forex, indices, metals, crypto and all account types including cent accounts.

docs.title: v0.1
docs.text:
Initial release

Core EA structure with Main Panel (account info, position summary, quick actions), dark theme UI, OnTimer-based refresh and OnTradeTransaction for instant updates.

docs.title: Does the EA place trades automatically?
docs.text: No. The EA is a trading panel, not an automated strategy. It helps you place, manage and close orders manually with greater speed and precision. All trading decisions are yours.

docs.title: Does it work on live accounts?
docs.text: Yes. The EA has been tested on both demo and live accounts across multiple brokers. Always test on a demo account first before using it on a live or funded account.

docs.title: Is it compatible with prop firm accounts?
docs.text: Yes, it has been tested on prop firm environments. However, always verify that your prop firm allows the use of trading panels and EAs before using it on a funded account. The EA does not use any forbidden techniques (no martingale, no grid, no tick scalping).

docs.title: Does it work on all instruments?
docs.text: Yes. The EA auto-detects the instrument type (Forex, indices, metals, crypto, CFDs) and adjusts lot size calculations accordingly. It works on any symbol available in your broker.

docs.title: Can I run multiple instances on the same account?
docs.text: Yes, but each instance must have a different Magic Number (InpMagicNumber). If two instances share the same magic number, they will interfere with each other's order tracking.

docs.title: The panel does not appear after loading the EA
docs.text:
Check the following:

  1. AutoTrading is enabled — the AutoTrading button in the MT5 toolbar must be green
  2. Algo Trading is allowed — right-click the chart → Expert Advisors → Properties → Common tab → check "Allow Algo Trading"
  3. The .ex5 file is in the correct folder — it must be placed in MQL5/Experts/ and MT5 must be refreshed (right-click the Navigator panel → Refresh)
  4. The chart has enough space — try widening the chart window if the panel appears cut off

docs.title: Orders are rejected with "Invalid stops" error
docs.text:
This means your SL or TP is too close to the current price for the broker's minimum stop distance. Solutions:

  • Increase InpSLTPBufferPts (try 3 or 5) in the EA properties
  • Enable InpShowStopsZone to see the broker's minimum distance visually on the chart
  • Move your SL or TP further from the entry price

docs.title: The lot size shown is 0.00
docs.text: This can happen on instruments with a very small volume step (e.g. BTCUSD with step 0.001). Make sure you are using v1.81 or later, which fixes this display issue.

docs.title: The EA settings reset after restarting MT5
docs.text:
All panel settings are saved automatically and restored on restart. If settings are not persisting, check that:

  • MT5 has write permissions to its data folder
  • The EA is not being removed and re-added (which triggers a clean state)
  • You are loading the EA on the same chart (settings are saved per chart ID)

docs.title: What is the difference between Manual and One-Click mode?
docs.text:
• Manual mode — you drag SL, TP and Entry lines on the chart to define your trade, then click Place Order. Best for precise setups with specific price levels.
  • One-Click mode — you set a fixed volume, SL and TP distance, then click BUY or SELL for an instant market order. Best for fast entries.

docs.title: The draggable lines are not showing
docs.text: Click the Activate lines button at the bottom of the Easy Order panel. Lines only appear when activated to avoid cluttering the chart when you are not actively placing an order.

docs.title: Can I use multi-TP on a Netting account?
docs.text: Yes. On Netting accounts, multi-TP is handled via the Synthetic Netting Ladder: the EA places a single order with the farthest TP as the real broker TP, then automatically executes partial closes at each intermediate rung. The result is the same as multi-TP from the user's perspective.

docs.title: What does the "runner" TP leg mean?
docs.text: When you activate multiple TP legs but leave one without a TP price (0%), that leg becomes a runner — a position that stays open indefinitely until you close it manually. This is useful for letting part of your position run with the trend while taking profit on the other legs.

docs.title: The Order List shows orders from other symbols
docs.text: By default the Order List shows all positions across your account. Use the symbol dropdown (top left of the panel) to filter by a specific instrument. The filter is saved and restored across restarts.

docs.title: Bulk actions are not working
docs.text:
Check that:

  • The view tab matches what you want to close (Open / Pending / All)
  • The symbol filter is set correctly (it may be filtering to a symbol with no orders)
  • AutoTrading is enabled in MT5

docs.title: What does the proximity highlight mean in the Order List?
docs.text: When the current price is within a certain percentage of an SL, TP or Entry level, that level is highlighted to warn you it may be hit soon. You can adjust the threshold with InpNearPct in the EA properties.

docs.title: The badges are not visible on the chart
docs.text: Make sure InpShowBadges is set to true in the EA properties. Also check that the EA is active (not paused) in the Main Panel.

docs.title: The badge colors do not match my positions
docs.text: Badge colors are fully customizable in the [TB] section of the EA properties. Separate colors are available for BUY vs SELL and for open positions vs pending orders.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Before installing, make sure you have:

  • MetaTrader 5 (build 3000 or higher)
  • A demo or live account at any broker
  • AutoTrading and Algo Trading enabled in MT5

docs.title: Enabling AutoTrading & Algo Trading
docs.text:
The EA requires both AutoTrading and Algo Trading to be active before placing orders.

AutoTrading (toolbar button):

  1. In MT5, look at the top toolbar
  2. Click the AutoTrading button — it turns green when active
  3. If it stays grey, check that your broker allows automated trading

Algo Trading (per-chart permission):

  1. Right-click the chart where the EA is loaded
  2. Go to Expert Advisors → Properties (or press F7)
  3. In the Common tab, check Allow Algo Trading
  4. Click OK

Warning

Orders will be rejected if either AutoTrading or Algo Trading is disabled.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Download the compiled EA (.ex5 file) from the MQL5 Marketplace
  2. Copy it to your MT5 Experts folder:
    • In MT5, go to File → Open Data Folder
    • Navigate to MQL5 → Experts
    • Paste the .ex5 file there
  3. Restart MT5 or right-click the Navigator panel and select Refresh
  4. The EA will appear under Navigator → Expert Advisors

docs.title: Adding the EA to a Chart
docs.text:
1. Open any chart in MT5
  2. In the Navigator panel, expand Expert Advisors
  3. Double-click OrdersManager_All_In_One or drag it onto the chart
  4. The Properties window will open — configure your settings and click OK
  5. The panel will appear on the left side of the chart

Recommended first steps

  1. Set your preferred language in OPTIONS → GENERAL ([EN] [ES] [PT] [RU])
  2. Set your Magic Number in the EA properties ([GC] section)
  3. Make sure AutoTrading and Algo Trading are both enabled

docs.title: Account Types
docs.text:
The EA works on both Hedging and Netting accounts. Multi-TP is supported on both, adapting automatically to the account type.

Account Type Multi-TP How it works Hedging ✅ Up to 4 TPs Each TP closes an independent position Netting ✅ Up to 4 TPs Intermediate TPs via automatic partial closes

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
The EA has been tested on both Hedging and Netting account types across multiple brokers and prop firm environments.

Prop firm rules

Always verify that your prop firm allows EAs before using this tool on a funded account.

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: All EA settings are accessible via MT5 → EA Properties (press F7 on the chart). Settings are organized in 6 sections, each identified by a prefix.

docs.title: [GC] General Configuration
docs.text: Core settings that affect the overall behavior of the EA.

docs.title: InpLanguage — Panel interface language
docs.text:
Default: LANG_EN Valid values: LANG_EN, LANG_ES, LANG_PT, LANG_RU

Sets the language used across all three panels (Main Panel, Easy Order, Order List). You can also change the language at any time directly from the Main Panel under OPTIONS → GENERAL using the [EN] [ES] [PT] [RU] toggle buttons — no restart required. This setting defines the language shown on first launch.

docs.title: InpMagicNumber — EA magic number
docs.text:
Default: 202600185 Valid values: Any positive integer

A unique numeric identifier the EA stamps on every order it places. MT5 uses this number to distinguish orders from different EAs or manual trades.

Important

If you run more than one EA on the same account, each one must have a different magic number. Duplicate magic numbers cause EAs to interfere with each other's orders.

docs.title: InpUpdateMs — Panel refresh interval
docs.text:
Default: 250 Valid values: 100 – 1000 (milliseconds)

Controls how often the panels refresh their data (prices, P&L, positions). Lower values make the panels feel more responsive but use more CPU.

Value Feel 100 Very smooth, higher CPU usage 250 Recommended — good balance 500 Slightly delayed but very light

docs.title: [TB] Trade Badges
docs.text: Badges are visual overlays drawn directly on the chart next to each SL, TP and Entry line. They show price level, distance in points, R:R ratio, and quick action buttons (+SL, +TP, close) for every open position and pending order.

docs.title: InpShowBadges — Enable badges
docs.text:
Default: true Valid values: true, false

Master switch for all chart badges. Set to false if you prefer a clean chart without overlays. You can toggle this at any time by reloading the EA properties.

docs.title: InpBadgeSLAtr — ATR multiplier for +SL button
docs.text:
Default: 1.5 Valid values: Any positive decimal (e.g. 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0)

When you click the +SL quick-action button on a badge, the EA automatically places the SL at a distance of N × ATR(14) from the current price. ATR(14) is the Average True Range over the last 14 candles on the current timeframe.

Example: If ATR(14) = 20 pts and InpBadgeSLAtr = 1.5, the SL is placed 20 × 1.5 = 30 pts from the current price.

Tip

Use a smaller multiplier (e.g. 1.0) for tight SLs on low-volatility instruments, and a larger one (e.g. 2.5) for volatile instruments like Gold or indices.

docs.title: InpBadgeTPAtr — ATR multiplier for +TP button
docs.text:
Default: 2.5 Valid values: Any positive decimal (e.g. 1.5, 2.5, 3.0)

Same logic as InpBadgeSLAtr but applied to the +TP button. Setting this higher than InpBadgeSLAtr ensures a positive R:R by default (e.g. SL at 1.5× ATR, TP at 2.5× ATR = R:R of ~1:1.67).

docs.title: Color inputs — InpTBColor*
docs.text:
Eight color parameters control the appearance of badge and line colors, split by direction (BUY/SELL) and order state (open position / pending order).

Parameter What it colors InpTBColorEntryBuy Entry badge and line — BUY open position InpTBColorEntrySell Entry badge and line — SELL open position InpTBColorEntryBuyPending Entry badge and line — BUY pending order InpTBColorEntrySellPending Entry badge and line — SELL pending order InpTBColorSL SL badge and line — open position InpTBColorSLPending SL badge and line — pending order InpTBColorTP TP badge and line — open position InpTBColorTPPending TP badge and line — pending order

Tip

Separating open position colors from pending order colors lets you distinguish at a glance which levels are active and which are still waiting to trigger.

docs.title: [EO] Easy Order Interface
docs.text: Settings for the Easy Order panel, which handles visual order placement using draggable SL/TP/Entry lines on the chart.

docs.title: InpFeedbackMs — Confirmation message duration
docs.text:
Default: 250 Valid values: 500 – 5000 (milliseconds)

After placing an order, the EA shows a brief confirmation at the bottom of the Easy Order panel — a green ✓ Order placed or red ✗ Error - see log. This setting controls how many milliseconds that message stays visible before returning to the normal placement button.

Value Feel 500 Quick flash 1500 Comfortable reading time (recommended) 3000 Stays visible for 3 seconds

docs.title: InpShowStopsZone — Show minimum stops zone
docs.text:
Default: false Valid values: true, false

When enabled, the EA draws a shaded zone on the chart showing the broker's minimum allowed distance for SL and TP from the current price. Any SL or TP placed inside this zone will be rejected by the broker.

Tip

Enable this when trading instruments with wide minimum stop requirements (e.g. some CFDs or exotic pairs) to avoid placement errors.

docs.title: InpSLTPBufferPts — Extra buffer over broker minimum stops
docs.text:
Default: 0 Valid values: 0 – 50 (points)

Adds extra points on top of the broker's minimum SL/TP distance. The EA uses this buffer when calculating whether your lines are in a valid position.

When to use this

If you frequently see "Invalid stops" errors when placing orders (MT5 error 10016), increase this value in small steps (try 3 or 5). This happens when the broker's minimum stop distance changes rapidly during high volatility and the EA's calculation becomes slightly outdated.

docs.title: InpMaxSlippagePts — Maximum allowed slippage
docs.text:
Default: 0 Valid values: 0 – 100 (points), 0 = disabled

Protects against price moving significantly between when you click Place Order and when the broker executes it. If the price moves more than this many points during that window, the order is aborted and an error is shown.

Value Behavior 0 No check — order always executes regardless of slippage 5 Aborts if price moves more than 5 pts before execution 10 More tolerant — suitable for slower connections

Tip

Leave at 0 for most cases. Enable it (e.g. 5–10 pts) when trading during high-impact news events where price can spike unpredictably.

docs.title: InpEOLabelsRight — Label position on chart lines
docs.text:
Default: true Valid values: true (right), false (left)

Controls which side of the chart the SL/TP/Entry line labels appear on.

  • true — labels appear on the right side (near the price axis)
  • false — labels appear on the left side

Change this if the labels overlap with your chart indicators or price action on either side.

docs.title: InpEOBadgeFontPt — Badge font size
docs.text:
Default: 0 Valid values: 0 (auto), or any positive integer (e.g. 8, 10, 12)

Font size in points for the text inside the EO badges on the chart. 0 = automatic — the EA picks the best size based on your current chart zoom level. Set a fixed value if you want consistent badge size regardless of zoom.

docs.title: InpEOLineStyle — Line style
docs.text:
Default: STYLE_DOT Valid values: STYLE_SOLID, STYLE_DASH, STYLE_DOT, STYLE_DASHDOT, STYLE_DASHDOTDOT

Visual style of the draggable SL/TP/Entry lines drawn on the chart.

Warning

Non-solid styles (STYLE_DASH, STYLE_DOT, etc.) only render correctly when InpEOLineWidth is set to 1. With higher widths, MT5 forces all lines to appear solid regardless of this setting.

docs.title: InpEOLineWidth — Line width
docs.text:
Default: 1 Valid values: 1 – 5

Thickness of the SL/TP/Entry lines in pixels. 1 is the thinnest, 5 the thickest. See the note in InpEOLineStyle about non-solid styles.

docs.title: InpEOFontScale — Label font scale
docs.text:
Default: 1.2 Valid values: 0.7 – 2.0

Scales the font size of the text labels displayed on the SL/TP/Entry lines. This affects readability when your chart is small or zoomed out.

Value Use case 0.7 Compact — fits many labels on a small chart 1.0 Normal size 1.2 Slightly larger (default) 1.5 Large — easier to read on high-resolution monitors

docs.title: Color inputs — InpEOColor*
docs.text:
Eight color parameters for the Easy Order lines and badges on the chart, organized by line type and trade direction.

Parameter What it colors InpEOColorSlBuy SL line and badge for BUY orders InpEOColorSlSell SL line and badge for SELL orders InpEOColorTpBuy TP line and badge for BUY orders InpEOColorTpSell TP line and badge for SELL orders InpEOColorEntBuy Entry line and badge for BUY orders InpEOColorEntSell Entry line and badge for SELL orders InpEOColorInvalid Any line dragged to an invalid position (e.g. SL above price on a BUY) InpEODirBtnColor The BUY ↔ SELL direction toggle button in the panel

docs.title: [OC] One-Click Mode
docs.text: Settings for the One-Click sub-mode of Easy Order, which lets you place instant market orders with a single click using pre-configured volume and stops.

docs.title: InpOCDefaultVolume — Default lot size
docs.text:
Default: 0.01 Valid values: Any valid lot size for your broker (e.g. 0.01, 0.1, 1.0)

The lot size pre-filled when you open One-Click mode. You can change it directly in the panel before clicking BUY or SELL — this setting only controls the initial value shown.

docs.title: InpOCDefaultSL — Default Stop Loss distance
docs.text:
Default: 10.0 Valid values: Any positive number in points

Distance of the Stop Loss from the entry price in points, pre-filled when opening One-Click mode.

Example: On EURUSD with 5-digit pricing, 10 points = 1 pip.

docs.title: InpOCDefaultTP — Default Take Profit distance
docs.text:
Default: 20.0 Valid values: Any positive number in points

Distance of the Take Profit from the entry price in points, pre-filled when opening One-Click mode. The default 20.0 with InpOCDefaultSL = 10.0 gives an initial R:R of 1:2.

docs.title: InpPanelFontScale — Main panel font scale
docs.text:
Default: 1.05 Valid values: 0.8 – 2.0

Scales the font size of all text in the Main Panel. Increase this on high-resolution or large monitors; decrease it if the panel feels too large on a small screen.

Value Use case 1.0 Normal size 1.05 Slightly larger (default) 1.3 Comfortable for 2K monitors 1.5 Large — for 4K or accessibility

docs.title: [LO] Order List Interface
docs.text: Settings for the Order List panel, which shows all open positions and pending orders with live P&L, risk and quick action buttons.

docs.title: InpListFontScale — Order List font scale
docs.text:
Default: 1.2 Valid values: 0.8 – 2.0

Scales the font size of the Order List panel independently from the Main Panel. Useful if you want the list to be more readable without making the main panel larger.

docs.title: InpNearPct — Price proximity threshold
docs.text:
Default: 0.0015 Valid values: 0.0005 – 0.01 (as a decimal percentage)

Controls when an SL, TP or Entry badge is highlighted as "near price" in the Order List. When the current price is within this percentage distance of a level, that level is visually highlighted to warn you it may be hit soon.

Value Meaning 0.0005 Alert when price is within 0.05% of the level 0.0015 Alert when price is within 0.15% (default) 0.003 Alert when price is within 0.30%

Tip

Use a smaller value for tight scalping strategies and a larger value for swing trading where price typically has more room to move.

docs.title: InpConfirmCloseAll — Double-click confirmation for Close All
docs.text:
Default: true Valid values: true, false

When true, the Close All button requires two clicks to execute — the first click arms it, the second confirms. This prevents accidentally closing all your positions with a single misclick.

Warning

Setting this to false means a single click immediately closes all open positions and pending orders. Use with caution.

docs.title: InpSoundBulkClose — Sound on Close All
docs.text:
Default: true Valid values: true, false

Plays an audio alert when the Close All action is executed. Useful as an audible confirmation that all positions have been closed, especially when monitoring multiple charts.

docs.title: InpSoundClose — Sound on individual order close
docs.text:
Default: true Valid values: true, false

Plays an audio alert each time a single position or pending order is closed via the Order List action buttons (the × close button on each row).

docs.title: How updates work on MQL5 Marketplace
docs.text: Orders Manager One For All is distributed through the MQL5 Marketplace. Updates are not applied automatically — here is how the process works for both sellers and buyers.

docs.title: For users: how to update
docs.text:
When a new version is available, MetaTrader 5 notifies you directly inside the terminal with a notification badge.

To apply the update:

  1. In MT5, go to Tools → Market (or click the Market tab)
  2. Navigate to My Purchases
  3. Find Orders Manager One For All and click Update
  4. The new .ex5 file is downloaded and replaces the previous version automatically

Reload required

If the EA is already loaded on a chart when you update, you need to remove and re-add it to the chart for the new version to take effect. MT5 does not hot-reload running EAs.

Stay informed

Subscribe to the Telegram Channel for update announcements with a summary of what changed before you update.

docs.title: Update channels
docs.text: Channel Purpose 📢 Telegram Channel Announcements for every new version 📖 Changelog Full list of changes per version 💬 Telegram Group Questions about updates or compatibility

docs.title: Version numbering
docs.text:
The EA follows this versioning pattern:

  • v1.x — current series (incremental improvements and fixes)
  • The version is visible in the Common tab of the EA Properties window (press F7) and in the panel header tooltip

docs.title: Checking your current version
docs.text:
Two ways to check which version you have installed:

  1. EA Properties — right-click the chart → Expert Advisors → Properties (or press F7) → Common tab. The version appears in the description block.

  2. Diagnostics button — click [Diagnostics] in OPTIONS → the first line of the Journal output shows the exact version, build date and magic number.

docs.title: Compatibility between versions
docs.text:
Settings and panel state are saved automatically via MT5 GlobalVariables. They persist across version updates — you do not need to reconfigure the EA after updating.

If a new version changes the input parameters (adds, removes or renames inputs), MT5 will show the Properties window automatically on first load so you can review and confirm the new defaults.

docs.title: Will an update break my active positions?
docs.text: No. The EA does not hold positions — it is a trading panel. Updating while positions are open is safe; the positions remain with the broker regardless of whether the EA is running.

docs.title: What happens to my Netting Ladders after an update?
docs.text: Netting Ladder state is persisted to disk in MQL5/Files/EO_netting/. As long as the file format has not changed between versions (noted in the changelog when it does), ladders survive updates and are reloaded automatically.

docs.title: Can I roll back to a previous version?
docs.text: MQL5 Marketplace does not provide rollback through the UI. If you need a previous version for any reason, contact support via the Telegram Group.

docs.title: Easy Order Panel
docs.text: The Easy Order panel handles order placement. It has two modes: Manual (draggable lines on the chart) and One-Click (instant market orders). Toggle between modes using the button in the panel header.

docs.title: Step 1 — Configure your order
docs.text:
Direction: Select BUY or SELL.

Order type: Select Market, Limit or Stop.

Risk mode: Choose how the lot size is calculated:

Mode Description % Percentage of your account balance / equity / free margin LOT Fixed lot size entered manually USD Fixed monetary amount

Risk Basis: Applies when using % mode — choose whether to calculate from Balance, Equity or Free Margin.

docs.title: Step 2 — Activate lines
docs.text:
Click Activate lines to draw the SL, TP and Entry lines on the chart. Drag each line to your desired price level. The panel updates in real time showing estimated lots, risk amount and R:R.

Tip

You can also drag the SL and TP directly from the Trade Badges on the chart after a position is open.

docs.title: Step 3 — Set Take Profits
docs.text:
Up to 4 Take Profit levels are available. Each TP can be assigned a percentage of the total volume.

  • Click a TP button (TP1–TP4) to enable or disable that leg
  • Enter the percentage for each active leg in the field next to the button
  • The Total active TPs line shows the sum — it cannot exceed 100%
  • Leave one leg at 0% to create a runner (position with no TP)

Note

On Netting accounts, multi-TP is handled automatically via partial closes. The setup is identical from the user's perspective.

docs.title: Step 4 — Place the order
docs.text: Click Place Order. A green ✓ or red ✗ confirmation appears briefly. If the order is rejected, check the MT5 Journal for details.

docs.title: One-Click Mode
docs.text:
One-Click mode places instant market orders with a single button press.

  1. Set your volume using the [−] / [+] buttons or the volume step selector
  2. Configure SL and TP distance in points in the ACTIVATE section
  3. Click BUY or SELL — the order executes immediately

Tip

One-Click mode is ideal for fast entries during volatile moments when you don't have time to drag lines on the chart.

docs.title: Closing the panel
docs.text: Click the ✕ button in the panel header to close Easy Order. All your settings (mode, risk, direction, TP configuration) are saved and restored the next time you open the panel.

docs.title: Main Panel
docs.text: The Main Panel is docked to the left side of the chart and is always visible. It gives you a complete overview of your account and quick access to all actions.

docs.title: Account Card
docs.text:
Shows real-time account information:

  • Balance — total account balance
  • Equity — balance including open P&L
  • Spread — current spread in points
  • Mode — account margin mode (Hedging or Netting)
  • Leverage — account leverage ratio

docs.title: Total Summary
docs.text:
Shows the current state of your positions on the active chart symbol:

  • Open positions — number of open trades
  • Pending — number of pending orders
  • P&L Total — combined unrealized profit/loss

docs.title: Quick Actions
docs.text: Button Action Close All Closes all open positions and optionally cancels pending orders BE All Moves the SL of all open positions to breakeven 25% / 50% / 75% Partially closes all open positions at the selected ratio EO Opens / closes the Easy Order panel LO Opens / closes the Order List panel

docs.title: OPTIONS Section
docs.text: The OPTIONS section is collapsible. Click the ▲/▼ button next to the OPTIONS header to expand or collapse it.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Option Description Language Switch the panel language between EN / ES / PT / RU Include Pending Orders on Close All When ON, Close All also cancels pending orders Include Commissions with Breakeven When ON, BE All adds a commission offset to the breakeven price

docs.title: EasyOrder (EO)
docs.text: Option Description EO Labels: left side When ON, line labels appear on the left side of the chart

docs.title: Order List (LO)
docs.text: Option Description Include Commissions in Risk When ON, commission is included in the risk calculation Reverse Mode Controls SL placement when using Breakeven on a reversed position

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text:
The [Diagnostics] button at the bottom of the OPTIONS section prints a full troubleshooting snapshot to the MT5 Journal/Experts tab. Use it when reporting a bug or asking for support.

The output includes:

  • EA version, magic number, language and state
  • Account type, currency, broker, server and leverage (no balance or login)
  • Symbol info: digits, spread, tick value, contract size, volume limits, stops level
  • MT5 build, connection status and AutoTrading status
  • All open positions: ticket, type, lots, entry, SL, TP, P&L and magic
  • All pending orders: type (BUY_LIMIT, SELL_STOP, etc.), price, SL, TP and magic
  • Active Netting Ladders: entry, direction, volume, rung count and next target
  • Last error code

To share the log: in the Experts tab, right-click any row → Open to view the full log file, or select rows and use Copy to paste into a support message.

docs.title: Dragging
docs.text: The panel can be moved anywhere on the chart by clicking and dragging the header area. This works in both expanded and minimized states.

docs.title: Minimize
docs.text:
Click the − button in the top-right corner to minimize the panel. When minimized, the panel collapses into a small mini menu that shows:

Button Action + Expand the panel back to full size EO Open / close the Easy Order panel LO Open / close the Order List panel

The mini menu can also be dragged to any position on the chart, so you can keep it out of the way while still having quick access to all panels.

Tip

Even with the Main Panel minimized you have full access to Easy Order and Order List via the mini menu — no need to expand first.

docs.title: Order List Panel
docs.text: The Order List panel shows all your open positions and pending orders with live P&L, risk and quick action buttons for each row.

docs.title: Header Bar
docs.text:
The header shows a real-time account summary split into three sections:

Section Contents Account Summary Balance, Equity, Margin, Free Margin Accumulated Risk Open positions count, pending orders count, total risk Positions Open count, pending count, total P&L

docs.title: Symbol filter
docs.text: The dropdown on the left filters the list by instrument. Select a specific symbol or All to see your entire account. The selection persists across MT5 restarts.

docs.title: Type filter
docs.text: Filters by order direction: All, Buy, Sell, Buy Limit, Sell Limit, Buy Stop, Sell Stop. Resets automatically when switching tabs.

docs.title: View tabs
docs.text: Tab Shows All Open positions and pending orders Open Open positions only Pending Pending orders only

docs.title: Columns
docs.text: Column Description TYPE Order type and direction (BUY / SELL / BUY LMT / etc.) ASSET Instrument symbol LOTS Position size ENTRY Entry price SL Stop Loss level TP Take Profit level (or next Netting Ladder rung if active) R:R Risk:Reward ratio RISK Estimated risk in account currency P&L Current unrealized profit or loss ACTIONS Per-row action buttons

docs.title: Row Actions
docs.text:
Each row has the following action buttons:

Button Action BE Move SL to breakeven for this position REV Reverse the position direction 25% / 50% / 75% Partial close at the selected ratio ✕ Close the position or cancel the pending order

docs.title: Bulk Actions
docs.text:
The bulk action buttons in the filter row apply to all visible rows matching the active symbol and type filters:

Button Action 25% / 50% / 75% Partial close all open positions BE Move all open position SLs to breakeven ✕ Close all open positions / cancel all pending orders

Close All confirmation

By default, the ✕ bulk close button requires a double-click to execute. The first click arms the button (it turns amber), the second click confirms. You can disable this in the EA properties with InpConfirmCloseAll = false.

docs.title: Netting Ladder
docs.text: When a Netting Ladder is active on a position, the TP column shows the next rung price instead of the broker TP, with a ▸ indicator. This lets you track exactly which partial close will execute next.

docs.title: Closing the panel
docs.text: Click the ✕ button in the panel header to close the Order List. Filters, selected symbol and view tab are saved and restored automatically.
